explanation:
The input requirement states that at the end of an inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) in either PCV or PSV mode (PCVMode or PSVMode), and if inspiratory pause (inspiratoryPause) is not required, it should be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure (RM), provided the GUI has requested it (RMButton). 

To capture this, we express that globally (at all times): if we are at the end of an inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), in (PCVMode or PSVMode), inspiratoryPause is not set, and the GUI requests RM (RMButton), then it must be possible to activate the Recruitment Maneuver (RM). This is a liveness property indicating that RM can occur after those conditions are met, usually expressed as a 'finally' property after the trigger set.

Thus, we encode: 
- bool_exp1 := inspiratoryPhaseEnd & (PCVMode | PSVMode) & (!inspiratoryPause) & RMButton 
- bool_exp3 := RM 

Using template 14 (G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))): For every state, if the trigger conditions occur, then within N_DURATION steps it should be possible to activate RM.

N_DURATION is chosen as 3 (reasonably short, but can be adjusted).

The requirement is: when in either PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, and if inspiratory pause is not required, it must be possible (i.e., allowed, not blocked) to initiate a lung recruitment maneuver (RM) via GUI request (RMButton). We must capture that if (at the end of inspiration in PCV or PSV mode) and inspiratory pause is not set (i.e., !inspiratoryPauseButton), then triggering RM (by RMButton) is allowed.

The LTL property should state that whenever we are at the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and either PCV or PSV mode is selected, and the GUI has not set an inspiratory pause, then if the GUI requests an RM (RMButton), the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) will be (can be) initiated. This is best captured by:
G(((inspiratoryPhaseEnd & (PCVMode | PSVMode) & (!inspiratoryPauseButton)) & RMButton) -> RM);
So: whenever at inspiratory phase end, in a supported mode, and pause is not required, if RMButton is pressed, RM is activated.

Mapping to template: this fits template 40 (G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3)).
bool_exp1: inspiratoryPhaseEnd & (PCVMode | PSVMode)
bool_exp2: !inspiratoryPauseButton
bool_exp3: RMButton -> RM

(We combine RMButton and RM as RMButton -> RM, since requirement is about possibility/allowance, not forced activation without request.)
N_DURATION is not relevant for this template.

The requirement states that after the end of an inspiration in either PCV or PSV mode, it should be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM), but only if no inspiratory pause is required and if the GUI requests the maneuver. Translating this, we want: Whenever (1) a mode is PCV or PSV, (2) we are at the end of inspiration, and (3) inspiratoryPause is not set (i.e., not required), and (4) the GUI requests RM (modelled as RMButton), then it should be possible to activate RM. In LTL, we formalize this by stating: Globally, if (PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd and not inspiratoryPause and RMButton, then RM must be set. This captures the causality between the stated conditions and the possibility (requirement) to initiate the Recruitment Maneuver.

The requirement specifies that in either PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, and provided an inspiratory pause is not required, the operator should be able to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if requested by the GUI. This directly maps to a liveness property: whenever (PCV mode or PSV mode) is active, AND inspiration ends, AND inspiratory pause is not set, AND the GUI requests RM (RMButton), then it should be possible (i.e., allowed -- not blocked by system logic) for RM to become (be) active. This can best be captured as a response property: under the stated preconditions, RM must become true (enabled) in the next state.

The natural language requirement states that when the ventilator is in either PCV or PSV mode, at the end of an inspiration, and if no inspiratory pause is required, then it should be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure (RM), if it is requested by the GUI (RMButton). Breaking this down: 1) The relevant modes are PCVMode or PSVMode; 2) The requirement triggers at inspiratoryPhaseEnd (the end of an inspiration); 3) inspiratoryPause should NOT be set (so !inspiratoryPause); 4) RMButton must be pressed for RM to be initiated. Therefore, whenever we are in (PCVMode | PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd becomes true, and inspiratoryPause is NOT true, and RMButton is true, then RM should become active (RM=1). This condition is precisely captured as a response using template 49: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F bool_exp3)), with the conjunction capturing the triggering scenario and the response specifying that eventually RM becomes active. F is used for the possibility, not necessity, as per the requirement's use of "shall be possible".

The property requires that whenever the system enters the end of an inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd is true), and as long as inspiratoryPause (indicating an inspiratory pause is active) is NOT set, and the ventilation mode is either PCVMode or PSVMode, and the GUI requests a Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton is pressed), then the RM (Recruitment Maneuver) procedure should be able to start (RM becomes true) at that point. This LTL formula uses the template for an implication with a conjunction of preconditions—the end of inspiration, the absence of inspiratory pause, being in PCV or PSV mode, and GUI RM request—implying the system allows the RM procedure to be initiated. This captures requirements stated in the natural language regarding mode, timing (end of inspiration), GUI control, and pause restrictions.

The requirement states that in PCV and PSV mode, immediately at the end of an inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and provided inspiratory pause is not required (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is not set), it should be possible to initiate the lung recruitment maneuver (RM) procedure if requested by the GUI (RMButton). This can be represented in LTL as: Whenever the system is in either PCVMode or PSVMode, and the inspiratory phase ends, and inspiratoryPauseButton is not set, and the GUI requests RM (via RMButton), then RM must become true (the recruitment maneuver is initiated). This is a conditional response property over multiple enabling conditions and a triggering event that must result in activating RM. The chosen template ensures that whenever all the conditions hold, RM must also hold, capturing the requirement of enabling RM only in the right context (PCV/PSV, not during inspiratoryPause, on inspiratoryPhaseEnd, and if requested).

The property specifies that whenever the end of the inspiratory phase is detected (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND neither inspiratory pause is commanded by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton) nor the Recruitment Maneuver is already active (RM), but the Recruitment Maneuver request is issued by the GUI (RMButton), then the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) can be triggered. This matches the natural language's intent for PCV and PSV mode: at the end of inspiration, if inspiratory pause is not required, and the GUI demands a recruitment maneuver, the functionality should activate RM. For specificity, triggering is typically enabled by the GUI's RMButton. The duration N_DURATION is set to 10 as a concrete example, though in practice it should match system timing requirements.

The requirement states: when in PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration and there is NOT an inspiratory pause required, it must be possible to initiate the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if required by the GUI. In terms of the available atomic propositions, we use (PCVMode OR PSVMode) for mode, inspiratoryPhaseEnd for the end of inspiration, !inspiratoryPauseButton to indicate that inspiratory pause is not required (set by the GUI), and RMButton to indicate the GUI asks for the Recruitment Maneuver. The property requires RM can be initiated (i.e., RM becomes true) upon all these conditions. The most natural LTL captures that: whenever these conditions are met, the system will eventually activate RM (possibly in the next state, or as soon as possible). Thus, we select a response template that triggers RM when (in relevant mode) & end of inspiration & no inspiratory pause & RM required by GUI.

We use bool_exp1 = (PCVMode | PSVMode), bool_exp2 = (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton), and bool_exp3 = RM.

Template 31 matches: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> X bool_exp3). N_DURATION is unused.

This says: whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) and (at end of inspiration and no inspiratory pause and RM requested by GUI), then in the next state RM is initiated.

The specification requires that, in PCV or PSV ventilation mode, after each inspiratory phase and only when an inspiratory pause is not required, the system must allow a lung recruitment maneuver (RM) to be started if the GUI requests it. We capture this as: whenever the end of the inspiratory phase occurs (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the inspiratory pause is not requested (NOT inspiratoryPauseButton), and the mode is PCV or PSV (PCVMode OR PSVMode), and the GUI requests Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton), then the RM procedure should be possible (i.e., RM becomes true) in the next step. This is best mapped to a safety template where a specific event combination guarantees a response at the next time step. Template 41 (G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))) fits, where:
- bool_exp1: inspiratoryPhaseEnd (end of inspiration)
- bool_exp2: (PCVMode OR PSVMode) & (!inspiratoryPauseButton) & RMButton (in correct mode, pause not required, GUI requires RM)
- bool_exp3: RM (Recruitment maneuver starts in next step)
N_DURATION is not required for this template, so set as null.
